items.title: US’ 100% Tariff On Generic Drugs May Hit Malaysian Producers Hard: Galen Centre
items.summary: The Galen Centre says the US' 100% tariff on imported generic drugs, which will take effect in August 2028, may severely affect Malaysian drug makers exporting to that market. The US is Malaysia's second-biggest export destination for pharmaceuticals. The post US’ 100% Tariff On Generic Drugs May Hit Malaysian Producers Hard: Galen Centre appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Why Returning To Work Shouldn’t Mean The End Of Breastfeeding — Siew Li Teoh
items.summary: When national progressive policies are matched by a well-prepared, empowered mother and supported by a receptive employer, the path to long-term breastfeeding success becomes clear. The post Why Returning To Work Shouldn’t Mean The End Of Breastfeeding — Siew Li Teoh appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: The State Must Care, Not Coerce: A Critique Of Malaysia’s Senior Citizens Bill — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam
items.summary: Meaningful action requires courage, commitment, and the humility to admit that you cannot legislate what only love can provide. The post The State Must Care, Not Coerce: A Critique Of Malaysia’s Senior Citizens Bill — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Non-Cancer Elective Surgery Waiting Time Up To 13 Months In MOH
items.summary: Dr Dzul says the waiting time for elective surgery for non-cancer cases is up to 13 months in MOH facilities. Cardiothoracic surgery, paediatric surgery, and urology have longer waiting times. Wait times for elective surgery for cancer are up to six weeks. The post Non-Cancer Elective Surgery Waiting Time Up To 13 Months In MOH appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Stool Tests Cannot Replace Diagnostic, Preventive Colonoscopy: Specialists
items.summary: Colorectal surgeons say stool tests are for general screening and can't replace colonoscopy to diagnose colorectal cancer when patients have symptoms. Polyps can be removed before turning cancerous, making it both a diagnostic and preventive procedure. The post Stool Tests Cannot Replace Diagnostic, Preventive Colonoscopy: Specialists appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Rakan KKM Still Stalled, Dzulkefly Omits Launch Date
items.summary: Dr Dzul says implementation of Rakan KKM, which first received funding under Budget 2025 nearly two years ago, is still stalled due to bureaucracy, without specifying the next target launch date. One of the main hurdles is the need to comply with Act 586. The post Rakan KKM Still Stalled, Dzulkefly Omits Launch Date appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia Heading In US Direction With MediAsas: Expert
items.summary: Syed Aljunid says with MediAsas, Malaysia is moving in the direction of the US by wrongly using private health insurance as a health financing solution. Introducing MediAsas further breaks up the pool of resources; premiums are unaffordable for elderly. The post Malaysia Heading In US Direction With MediAsas: Expert appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: JPA: Only 46% Of Medical Officers Complete Specialisation On Time
items.summary: JPA says only 46% of doctors complete specialisation on time, thus delaying specialty training for others. JPA also approved over 66,000 new positions without trade-offs for MOH from 2019. The issue now is workforce distribution, not creating positions. The post JPA: Only 46% Of Medical Officers Complete Specialisation On Time appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MOH’s Nursing Workforce Contracted Since 2023 With 1,200 Nurses Lost
items.summary: Besides doctors, MOH's nursing workforce has also declined, losing 1,200 nurses across 2023 and 2024. The private sector gained 5,658 nurses in that two-year period. The 2023 contraction of MOH's nursing workforce was the first in five years since 2018. The post MOH’s Nursing Workforce Contracted Since 2023 With 1,200 Nurses Lost appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia’s Workplace Mental Health Strategy Must Be Family Centred — June Joseph
items.summary: Workplace mental health policy should be accompanied by a national strategy that integrates parental and child health, education, employment, and social welfare. The post Malaysia’s Workplace Mental Health Strategy Must Be Family Centred — June Joseph appeared first on CodeBlue .
